title: Anchuthengu Fort
tagline: Anchuthengu Fort — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: The fort was the East India Company's first permanent post on the Malabar Coast.
overview.description: The fort was the East India Company's first permanent post on the Malabar Coast. In November 1693, John Brabourne was sent by the British East India Company (EIC) to Attingal, where he obtained from Rani Ashure a grant of a site for a fort on the sandy spit of Anchuthengu (then known as Anjengo), together with the monopoly of the pepper trade of Attingal. The EIC commenced construction in January 1696. In June 1696 British pirates in the ketch Josiah under Robert Culliford, destroyed the Bengal Pilot Service's sloop Gingali (or Gingalle) at Anjengo.

topPlaces.name: Sarkaradevi Temple
topPlaces.description: Sarkaradevi Temple is one of the most important temples in South India. It is situated Chirayinkeezhu town in Thiruvananthapuram district.

topPlaces.name: Sarkara-Chirayinkeezhu
topPlaces.description: Sarkara-Chirayinkeezhu is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kadakkavoor
topPlaces.description: Kadakkavoor (also spelled Kadakkavur) is a village in Chirayinkeezhu taluk, Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India. It lies about 8 km north-west of Attingal, on the Thiruvananthapuram–Kollam section of the Southern Railway, and is a centre of the coir industry.

topPlaces.name: Vakkom
topPlaces.description: Vakkom is an area of the Thiruvananthapuram Metropolitan Area near Kadakkavoor in the Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India. It is 35 km north of Thiruvananthapuram, the state capital.

topPlaces.name: Koonthalloor
topPlaces.description: Kizhuvalam-Koonthalloor is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Akathumuri
topPlaces.description: Akathumuri is a scenic lakeside village and an education hub in Cheruniyoor panchayat of Varkala Taluk in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India. Village is situated 7 km (4.3 mi) south of Varkala city centre and 39 km (24 mi) north of Trivandrum City.

topPlaces.name: Keezhattingal (village)
topPlaces.description: Keezhattingal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Ponnumthuruthu
topPlaces.description: Ponnumthuruthu also known as Golden Island is an island in Varkala of Trivandrum district in Kerala.It is located between 'Panayilkadavu, Vakkom, Attingal'and 'Panayilkadavu, Vennicode, Varkala'.It is situated in Anjuthengu lake or called Panayilkadavu lake.The island is a property of the Valiyapurakkal Family. The island also contain a Hindu temple that is 100 years old.

items.note: Sarkaradevi Temple is one of the most important temples in South India. It is situated Chirayinkeezhu town in Thiruvananthapuram district.

Vakkom is an area of the Thiruvananthapuram Metropolitan Area near Kadakkavoor in the Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India. It is 35 km north of Thiruvananthapuram, the state capital.

Akathumuri is a scenic lakeside village and an education hub in Cheruniyoor panchayat of Varkala Taluk in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India. Village is situated 7 km (4.3 mi) south of Varkala city centre and 39 km (24 mi) north of Trivandrum City.

Ponnumthuruthu also known as Golden Island is an island in Varkala of Trivandrum district in Kerala.It is located between 'Panayilkadavu, Vakkom, Attingal'and 'Panayilkadavu, Vennicode, Varkala'.It is situated in Anjuthengu lake or called Panayilkadavu lake.The island is a property of the Valiyapurakkal Family. The island also contain a Hindu temple that is 100 years old.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Anchuthengu Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Anchuthengu Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Anchuthengu Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (28 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Anchuthengu Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Anchuthengu Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Anchuthengu Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Anchuthengu Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Anchuthengu Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sarkaradevi Temple, Sarkara-Chirayinkeezhu, Kadakkavoor, Vakkom, Koonthalloor.
